meta.domain_hint: Generic protocol change rules for structured messages/packets (protocol-agnostic)

changes.change_condition: When an endpoint sends an ACK frame
changes.new_value: the delay intentionally introduced between the time the packet with the largest packet number is received and the time an acknowledgment is sent
changes.evidence: The endpoint encodes this acknowledgment delay in the ACK Delay field of an ACK frame

changes.change_condition: When buffering delays are caused by unavailability of decryption keys
changes.new_value: buffering delays caused by unavailability of decryption keys are included
changes.evidence: However, endpoints SHOULD include buffering delays caused by unavailability of decryption keys

changes.change_condition: When decoding the ACK Delay field in the ACK frame
changes.new_value: decoded using ack_delay_exponent as an exponent
changes.evidence: The acknowledgment delay exponent is an integer value indicating an exponent used to decode the ACK Delay field in the ACK frame

changes.change_condition: When populating the field in an ACK frame
changes.change_action: must not include delays that the endpoint does not control
changes.evidence: An endpoint MUST NOT include delays that it does not control when populating the ACK Delay field in an ACK frame.

changes.change_condition: When the measured acknowledgment delay is larger than max_ack_delay
changes.old_value: measured acknowledgment delay larger than max_ack_delay
changes.new_value: reported ACK Delay reflects the measured delay
changes.evidence: When the measured acknowledgment delay is larger than its max_ack_delay, an endpoint SHOULD report the measured delay.

changes.change_condition: when decoding the acknowledgment delay in the ACK frame
changes.new_value: value in the field multiplied by 2 to the power of the ack_delay_exponent transport parameter
changes.evidence: It is decoded by multiplying the value in the field by 2 to the power of the ack_delay_exponent transport parameter sent by the sender of the ACK frame

changes.change_condition: when sending ACK frames
changes.new_value: the acknowledgment delay from the largest acknowledged packet
changes.evidence: ACK frames carry the most recent set of acknowledgments and the acknowledgment delay from the largest acknowledged packet

changes.change_condition: if any computed packet number is negative
changes.evidence: If any computed packet number is negative, an endpoint MUST generate a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

changes.change_condition: for ACK Ranges in the ACK frame
changes.new_value: the number of Gap and ACK Range Length values is determined by the ACK Range Count field; one of each value is present for each value in the ACK Range Count field
changes.evidence: The number of Gap and ACK Range Length values is determined by the ACK Range Count field; one of each value is present for each value in the ACK Range Count field.

changes.change_condition: if an ACK frame has more acknowledgment ranges than the remainder of the packet could carry
changes.evidence: FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR (0x07):  An endpoint received a frame that was badly formatted -- for instance, a frame of an unknown type or an ACK frame that has more acknowledgment ranges than the remainder of the packet could carry.

changes.change_condition: given a largest packet number for the range
changes.evidence: Thus, given a largest packet number for the range, the smallest value is determined by the following formula: smallest = largest - ack_range

changes.change_condition: when ACK Range Length has value 0
changes.new_value: only the largest packet number is acknowledged
changes.evidence: A value of 0 indicates that only the largest packet number is acknowledged.

changes.change_condition: A receiver of packets on multiple paths
changes.related_state_or_step: sending ACK frames during migration period
changes.evidence: A receiver of packets on multiple paths will still send ACK frames covering all received packets.

changes.change_condition: After receiving acknowledgments for an ACK frame
changes.old_value: those acknowledged ACK Ranges are tracked
changes.new_value: those acknowledged ACK Ranges are no longer tracked
changes.related_state_or_step: receiver processing acknowledgments for an ACK frame
changes.evidence: After receiving acknowledgments for an ACK frame, the receiver SHOULD stop tracking those acknowledged ACK Ranges.

changes.change_condition: If retaining many ACK Ranges could cause an ACK frame to become too large, including if an ACK frame would be too large to fit in a packet
changes.change_action: discard unacknowledged values to limit ACK frame size
changes.new_value: some unacknowledged ACK Ranges are discarded
changes.evidence: A receiver can discard unacknowledged ACK Ranges to limit ACK frame size... This is necessary if an ACK frame would be too large to fit in a packet.

changes.change_condition: Unless the receiver can ensure that it will not subsequently accept packets with numbers in that range
changes.related_state_or_step: receiver deciding whether to discard an ACK Range
changes.evidence: A receiver MUST retain an ACK Range unless it can ensure that it will not subsequently accept packets with numbers in that range.

changes.change_condition: When choosing to discard all remembered ranges
changes.evidence: Receivers can discard all ACK Ranges, but they MUST retain the largest packet number that has been successfully processed

changes.change_condition: if an ACK frame does not fit within a single QUIC packet
changes.change_action: omit older ranges with the smallest packet numbers
changes.evidence: If it does not, then older ranges (those with the smallest packet numbers) are omitted.

changes.change_condition: when an ACK frame is sent
changes.change_action: must include one or more ranges
changes.new_value: one or more ranges of acknowledged packets
changes.evidence: When an ACK frame is sent, one or more ranges of acknowledged packets are included.

changes.change_condition: when constructing ACK frames
changes.change_action: should acknowledge the most recently received packets
changes.evidence: ACK frames SHOULD always acknowledge the most recently received packets

changes.change_condition: If this value is absent
changes.related_state_or_step: decode the ACK Delay field in the ACK frame
changes.evidence: If this value is absent, a default value of 3 is assumed (indicating a multiplier of 8).

changes.change_condition: When the value is above 20
changes.evidence: Values above 20 are invalid.

changes.change_condition: client processing remembered transport parameters for a new connection using 0-RTT
changes.change_action: must not use remembered value; must use server's new value from the handshake, or default value if no new value is provided
changes.new_value: server's new value in the handshake or default value
changes.related_state_or_step: 0-RTT transport parameter use before and after handshake
changes.evidence: A client MUST NOT use remembered values for the following parameters: ack_delay_exponent, max_ack_delay, initial_source_connection_id, original_destination_connection_id, preferred_address, retry_source_connection_id, and stateless_reset_token.  The client MUST use the server's new values in the handshake instead; if the server does not provide new values, the default values are used.

changes.change_condition: CONNECTION_CLOSE frame has type 0x1d
changes.new_value: codes defined by the application protocol
changes.evidence: A CONNECTION_CLOSE frame of type 0x1d uses codes defined by the application protocol; see Section 20.2.

changes.change_condition: If an application is no longer interested in the data it is receiving on a stream
changes.evidence: it can abort reading the stream and specify an application error code.

changes.change_condition: When QUIC closes the connection after the application protocol has arranged to close a connection
changes.evidence: When QUIC consequently closes the connection, a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame with an application-supplied error code will be used to signal closure to the peer.

changes.change_condition: When sending a RESET_STREAM frame in response to a STOP_SENDING frame
changes.change_action: may be set to any application error code
changes.evidence: but it can use any application error code.

changes.change_condition: When an endpoint that receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame sends a single packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame before entering the draining state, if appropriate
changes.evidence: An endpoint that receives a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame MAY send a single packet containing a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame before entering the draining state, using a NO_ERROR code if appropriate.
changes.note: The text says 'NO_ERROR code' without specifying whether the CONNECTION_CLOSE carries application or transport error space.

changes.change_condition: For provisional registrations
changes.change_action: must equal one of listed values
changes.evidence: Status:  "permanent" or "provisional".
changes.note: Catalog does not contain a Status field; mapped approximately to Code with incomplete information.

changes.evidence:
|0x0100-| CRYPTO_ERROR              |TLS alert code  |Section 20    |
|0x01ff |                           |                |              |

changes.change_condition: when Code is APPLICATION_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x0c   | APPLICATION_ERROR         |Application     |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: when Code is CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x0d   | CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED    |CRYPTO data     |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: when Code is FINAL_SIZE_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry

changes.change_condition: when Code is FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x03   | FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR        |Flow control    |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: when Code is FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x07   | FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR      |Frame encoding  |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: when Code is INTERNAL_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x01   | INTERNAL_ERROR            |Implementation  |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: when Code is TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR in the initial contents of this registry
changes.evidence: |0x08   | TRANSPORT_PARAMETER_ERROR |Error in        |Section 20    |

changes.change_condition: for an Initial packet sent by the client after Retry handling
changes.change_action: must equal previous value / must use same content
changes.new_value: same cryptographic handshake message it included in this packet
changes.related_state_or_step: Initial packet sent by the client
changes.evidence: A client MUST use the same cryptographic handshake message it included in this packet.

changes.change_condition: if a packet contains a different cryptographic handshake message
changes.evidence: A server MAY treat a packet that contains a different cryptographic handshake message as a connection error or discard it.

changes.change_condition: when carrying error codes specific to the cryptographic handshake
changes.evidence: CRYPTO_ERROR (0x0100-0x01ff):  The cryptographic handshake failed.  A range of 256 values is reserved for carrying error codes specific to the cryptographic handshake that is used.

changes.change_condition: For every PATH_CHALLENGE frame sent.
changes.evidence: The endpoint MUST use unpredictable data in every PATH_CHALLENGE frame so that it can associate the peer's response with the corresponding PATH_CHALLENGE.

changes.change_condition: If data is sent multiple times at the same offset within a stream
changes.old_value: previous data at a given offset
changes.new_value: different data at the same offset
changes.evidence: The data at a given offset MUST NOT change if it is sent multiple times;

changes.change_condition: If different data is received at the same offset within a stream
changes.old_value: data previously received at that offset
changes.new_value: different data at the same offset
changes.evidence: an endpoint MAY treat receipt of different data at the same offset within a stream as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.
changes.note: error type is outside the provided catalog

changes.change_condition: On receiving a PATH_CHALLENGE frame.
changes.new_value: echo of the data contained in the PATH_CHALLENGE frame
changes.evidence: On receiving a PATH_CHALLENGE frame, an endpoint MUST respond by echoing the data contained in the PATH_CHALLENGE frame in a PATH_RESPONSE frame.

changes.change_condition: To initiate path validation, an endpoint sends a PATH_CHALLENGE frame.
changes.evidence: To initiate path validation, an endpoint sends a PATH_CHALLENGE frame containing an unpredictable payload on the path to be validated.

changes.change_condition: When a PATH_RESPONSE frame is received for successful path validation.
changes.new_value: the data that was sent in a previous PATH_CHALLENGE frame
changes.evidence: Path validation succeeds when a PATH_RESPONSE frame is received that contains the data that was sent in a previous PATH_CHALLENGE frame.

changes.change_condition: content of a PATH_RESPONSE frame does not match the content of a PATH_CHALLENGE frame previously sent by the endpoint
changes.evidence: If the content of a PATH_RESPONSE frame does not match the content of a PATH_CHALLENGE frame previously sent by the endpoint, the endpoint MAY generate a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.

changes.change_condition: each time PATH_CHALLENGE frames are sent
changes.evidence: PATH_CHALLENGE frames include a different payload each time they are sent.
changes.note: The chunk says payload, mapped to catalog field 'data' with incomplete information.

changes.change_condition: recipient generates a PATH_RESPONSE frame in response to a PATH_CHALLENGE frame
changes.new_value: same Data value as received in PATH_CHALLENGE
changes.evidence: The recipient of this frame MUST generate a PATH_RESPONSE frame containing the same Data value.

changes.change_condition: permanent registrations in this registry
changes.evidence: permanent registrations in this registry MUST include the following fields: ... Description

changes.change_condition: For this transport parameter
changes.evidence: This parameter is a zero-length value.

changes.change_condition: server selects a smaller value than the previously sent value
changes.related_state_or_step: server recovery and comparison of stored transport parameters for 0-RTT
changes.evidence: A server MAY store and recover the previously sent values of the max_idle_timeout, max_udp_payload_size, and disable_active_migration parameters and reject 0-RTT if it selects smaller values.

changes.change_condition: An endpoint receives an ACK frame with ECN counts
changes.change_action: validated by comparing newly received counts against those from the last successfully processed ACK frame
changes.old_value: counts from the last successfully processed ACK frame
changes.related_state_or_step: 13.4.2.1 Receiving ACK Frames with ECN Counts
changes.evidence: It performs this validation by comparing newly received counts against those from the last successfully processed ACK frame.

changes.change_condition: Any increase in the ECN counts
changes.change_action: must be validated based on ECN markings applied to newly acknowledged packets
changes.related_state_or_step: 13.4.2.1 Receiving ACK Frames with ECN Counts
changes.evidence: Any increase in the ECN counts is validated based on the ECN markings that were applied to packets that are newly acknowledged in the ACK frame.

changes.change_condition: If an ACK frame newly acknowledges a packet that the endpoint sent with either the ECT(0) or ECT(1) codepoint set, and the corresponding ECN counts are not present in the ACK frame
changes.related_state_or_step: 13.4.2.1 Receiving ACK Frames with ECN Counts
changes.evidence: ECN validation fails if the corresponding ECN counts are not present in the ACK frame.

changes.change_condition: frame type is 0x03
changes.evidence: If the frame type is 0x03, ACK frames also contain the cumulative count of QUIC packets with associated ECN marks received on the connection up until this point.

changes.change_condition: subsequent ACK frames after ECN markings are received
changes.evidence: These ECN counts are included in subsequent ACK frames; see Sections 13.2 and 19.3.

changes.change_condition: to start testing a path
changes.evidence:
To start testing a path, the ECN state is set to "testing", and
   existing ECN counts are remembered as a baseline.

changes.change_condition: when validating against newly acknowledged packets in an ACK frame
changes.change_action: validated range check; may be larger than the total number of packets that are acknowledged
changes.evidence: This is why ECN counts are permitted to be larger than the total number of packets that are acknowledged.

changes.change_condition: when validating support for ECN using ACK frames acknowledging the first packets sent
changes.change_action: validated presence check; support is validated by observing that ACK frames carry ECN counts
changes.related_state_or_step: ECN support validation during the handshake
changes.evidence: An endpoint validates support for ECN by observing whether the ACK frames acknowledging the first packets it sends carry ECN counts

changes.change_condition: If an endpoint has cause to expect that IP packets with an ECT codepoint might be dropped by a faulty network element
changes.change_action: could set an ECT codepoint for only the first ten outgoing packets on a path, or for a period of three PTOs
changes.new_value: ECT codepoint on limited outgoing packets
changes.evidence: the endpoint could set an ECT codepoint for only the first ten outgoing packets on a path, or for a period of three PTOs

changes.change_condition: To perform ECN validation for a new path
changes.evidence: The endpoint sets an ECT(0) codepoint in the IP header of early outgoing packets sent on a new path to the peer [RFC8311].

changes.change_condition: if validation fails
changes.change_action: set to not use ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends
changes.old_value: setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets
changes.evidence: If validation fails, then the endpoint MUST disable ECN.  It stops setting the ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends

changes.change_condition: if validation later fails
changes.change_action: set to not use ECT codepoint in IP packets that it sends
changes.evidence: an endpoint MUST disable ECN if validation later fails.

changes.change_condition: on paths not in a "testing" or "capable" state
changes.evidence:
On paths with a "testing" or
   "capable" state, the endpoint sends packets with an ECT marking --
   ECT(0) by default; otherwise, the endpoint sends unmarked packets.

changes.change_condition: on paths with a "testing" or "capable" state
changes.change_action: set to ECT marking by default
changes.evidence:
On paths with a "testing" or
   "capable" state, the endpoint sends packets with an ECT marking --
   ECT(0) by default; otherwise, the endpoint sends unmarked packets.

changes.change_condition: upon successful validation
changes.change_action: may continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends
changes.evidence: Upon successful validation, an endpoint MAY continue to set an ECT codepoint in subsequent packets it sends

changes.change_condition: Coalesced QUIC packets share the same IP header
changes.change_action: increment once for each coalesced QUIC packet
changes.evidence: Coalesced QUIC packets (see Section 12.2) share the same IP header so the ECN counts are incremented once for each coalesced QUIC packet.

changes.change_condition: On receiving an IP packet with an ECN-CE codepoint
changes.evidence: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE codepoint, an ECN-enabled endpoint accesses the ECN field and increases the corresponding ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE count.

changes.change_condition: duplicate QUIC packets are not processed
changes.evidence: ECN counts are only incremented when QUIC packets from the received IP packet are processed. As such, duplicate QUIC packets are not processed and do not increase ECN counts;

changes.change_condition: if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking
changes.evidence: ECN validation also fails if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking.

changes.change_condition: if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking
changes.evidence: Similarly, ECN validation fails if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking.

changes.change_condition: when ECN counts are present
changes.evidence: When present, there are three ECN counts, as shown in Figure 27.

changes.change_condition: Coalesced QUIC packets share the same IP header
changes.change_action: increment once for each coalesced QUIC packet
changes.evidence: Coalesced QUIC packets (see Section 12.2) share the same IP header so the ECN counts are incremented once for each coalesced QUIC packet.

changes.change_condition: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0) codepoint
changes.evidence: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE codepoint, an ECN-enabled endpoint accesses the ECN field and increases the corresponding ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE count.

changes.change_condition: duplicate QUIC packets are not processed
changes.evidence: ECN counts are only incremented when QUIC packets from the received IP packet are processed. As such, duplicate QUIC packets are not processed and do not increase ECN counts;

changes.change_condition: if the received total count for ECT(0) exceeds the total number of packets sent with the corresponding ECT codepoint
changes.evidence: ECN validation can fail if the received total count for either ECT(0) or ECT(1) exceeds the total number of packets sent with each corresponding ECT codepoint.

changes.change_condition: if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking
changes.evidence: ECN validation also fails if the sum of the increase in ECT(0) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets that were originally sent with an ECT(0) marking.

changes.change_condition: when ECN counts are present
changes.evidence: When present, there are three ECN counts, as shown in Figure 27.

changes.change_condition: when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied
changes.evidence: In particular, validation will fail when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied.

changes.change_condition: Coalesced QUIC packets share the same IP header
changes.change_action: increment once for each coalesced QUIC packet
changes.evidence: Coalesced QUIC packets (see Section 12.2) share the same IP header so the ECN counts are incremented once for each coalesced QUIC packet.

changes.change_condition: Implementations that use the ECT(1) codepoint
changes.new_value: reported ECT(1) counts used in ECN validation
changes.evidence: Implementations that use the ECT(1) codepoint need to perform ECN validation using the reported ECT(1) counts.

changes.change_condition: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(1) codepoint
changes.evidence: On receiving an IP packet with an ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE codepoint, an ECN-enabled endpoint accesses the ECN field and increases the corresponding ECT(0), ECT(1), or ECN-CE count.

changes.change_condition: duplicate QUIC packets are not processed
changes.evidence: ECN counts are only incremented when QUIC packets from the received IP packet are processed. As such, duplicate QUIC packets are not processed and do not increase ECN counts;

changes.change_condition: if the received total count for ECT(1) exceeds the total number of packets sent with the corresponding ECT codepoint
changes.evidence: ECN validation can fail if the received total count for either ECT(0) or ECT(1) exceeds the total number of packets sent with each corresponding ECT codepoint.

changes.change_condition: if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking
changes.evidence: Similarly, ECN validation fails if the sum of the increases to ECT(1) and ECN-CE counts is less than the number of newly acknowledged packets sent with an ECT(1) marking.

changes.change_condition: when ECN counts are present
changes.evidence: When present, there are three ECN counts, as shown in Figure 27.

changes.change_condition: when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied
changes.evidence: In particular, validation will fail when an endpoint receives a non-zero ECN count corresponding to an ECT codepoint that it never applied.

changes.change_condition: After sending a STOP_SENDING frame, upon receiving RESET_STREAM frames for that stream
changes.old_value: error code in received RESET_STREAM frames
changes.evidence: An endpoint that sends a STOP_SENDING frame MAY ignore the error code in any RESET_STREAM frames subsequently received for that stream.

changes.change_condition: CONNECTION_CLOSE frame has type 0x1c
changes.new_value: codes from the space defined in Section 20.1
changes.evidence: A CONNECTION_CLOSE frame of type 0x1c uses codes from the space defined in Section 20.1.

changes.change_condition: If an endpoint's buffer is exceeded during the handshake and it does not expand its buffer
changes.evidence: If an endpoint does not expand its buffer, it MUST close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

changes.change_condition: Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame and chooses to close the connection
changes.evidence: Once the handshake completes, if an endpoint is unable to buffer all data in a CRYPTO frame, it MAY discard that CRYPTO frame and all CRYPTO frames received in the future, or it MAY close the connection with a CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED error code.

changes.change_condition: When sending a RESET_STREAM frame in response to a STOP_SENDING frame
changes.new_value: error code from the STOP_SENDING frame
changes.evidence: An endpoint SHOULD copy the error code from the STOP_SENDING frame to the RESET_STREAM frame it sends

changes.change_condition: application wishes to abandon a connection during the handshake
changes.related_state_or_step: sending CONNECTION_CLOSE frame (type 0x1c) in an Initial or Handshake packet
changes.evidence: When an application wishes to abandon a connection during the handshake, an endpoint can send a CONNECTION_CLOSE frame (type 0x1c) with an error code of APPLICATION_ERROR in an Initial or Handshake packet.

changes.change_condition: for QUIC transport error codes and application error codes
changes.evidence: QUIC transport error codes and application error codes are 62-bit unsigned integers.

changes.change_condition: if an application-level error affects a single stream but otherwise leaves the connection in a recoverable state
changes.change_action: set to an appropriate error code
changes.related_state_or_step: send a RESET_STREAM frame to terminate just the affected stream
changes.evidence: the endpoint can send a RESET_STREAM frame (Section 19.4) with an appropriate error code to terminate just the affected stream.

changes.change_condition: when an endpoint detects an error condition
changes.change_action: may be selected from any applicable error code
changes.evidence: In particular, an endpoint MAY use any applicable error code when it detects an error condition; a generic error code (such as PROTOCOL_VIOLATION or INTERNAL_ERROR) can always be used in place of specific error codes.

changes.change_condition: when signaling an error in a frame
changes.evidence: The most appropriate error code (Section 20) SHOULD be included in the frame that signals the error.

changes.change_condition: Example structure definition
changes.evidence: Field With Maximum Length (..128)

changes.change_condition: Example structure definition
changes.evidence: Field With Minimum Length (16..)

changes.change_condition: Example structure definition
changes.change_action: must be encoded as variable-length integer
changes.evidence: Field with Variable-Length Integer (i)

changes.change_condition: when encoded with 2MSB 00
when encoded with 2MSB 01
when encoded with 2MSB 10
when encoded with 2MSB 11
After the application indicates that all stream data has been sent and a STREAM frame is sent
changes.evidence: After the application indicates that all stream data has been sent and a STREAM frame containing the FIN bit is sent, the sending part of the stream enters the "Data Sent" state.

changes.change_condition: CRYPTO frames
changes.evidence: The stream does not have an explicit end, so CRYPTO frames do not have a FIN bit.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame marks the end of the stream
changes.change_action: must equal 1 to indicate end of stream
changes.evidence: The FIN bit (0x01) indicates that the frame marks the end of the stream.

changes.change_condition: When a STREAM frame is received and it has the FIN bit
changes.change_action: validated as indicating final size is known
changes.evidence: When a STREAM frame with a FIN bit is received, the final size of the stream is known; see Section 4.5.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame with FIN bit
changes.new_value: sum of the offset and the length of this frame
changes.evidence: The final size of the stream is the sum of the offset and the length of this frame.

changes.change_condition: When a STREAM frame with a FIN bit is received
changes.evidence: When a STREAM frame with a FIN bit is received, the final size of the stream is known; see Section 4.5. The receiving part of the stream then enters the "Size Known" state.

changes.change_condition: after a final size for a stream is known
changes.evidence: Once a final size for a stream is known, it cannot change.

changes.change_condition: alternatively in RESET_STREAM
changes.evidence: Alternatively, the Final Size field of a RESET_STREAM frame carries this value.

changes.change_condition: for determining the stream final size
changes.new_value: the sum of the Offset and Length fields of a STREAM frame with a FIN flag
changes.evidence: The final size is the sum of the Offset and Length fields of a STREAM frame with a FIN flag, noting that these fields might be implicit.

changes.change_condition: if a RESET_STREAM or STREAM frame is received indicating a change in the final size for the stream

If 0-RTT data is accepted by the server, the server MUST NOT reduce any limits or alter any values that might be violated by the client with its 0-RTT data.  In particular, a server that accepts 0-RTT data MUST NOT set values for the following parameters (Section 18.2) that are smaller than the remembered values of the parameters.

*  active_connection_id_limit

*  initial_max_data

*  initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local

The applicable subset of transport parameters that permit the sending of application data SHOULD be set to non-zero values for 0-RTT.  This includes initial_max_data and either (1) initial_max_streams_bidi and initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote or (2) initial_max_streams_uni and initial_max_stream_data_uni.

changes.evidence: all ack-eliciting 0-RTT and 1-RTT packets within its advertised max_ack_delay

changes.change_condition: when a sender wishes to send data but is unable to do so due to connection-level flow control
changes.new_value: the connection-level limit at which blocking occurred
changes.evidence: Maximum Data:  A variable-length integer indicating the connection- level limit at which blocking occurred.

changes.change_condition: when determining how large a limit is advertised
changes.change_action: small changes are undesirable if sent frequently; larger increments to limits are necessary if updates are less frequent
changes.evidence: Therefore, frequently sending MAX_STREAM_DATA and MAX_DATA frames with small changes is undesirable.  On the other hand, if updates are less frequent, larger increments to limits are necessary to avoid blocking a sender, requiring larger resource commitments at the receiver.

changes.change_condition: when interpreting initial_max_data
changes.change_action: set initial value for maximum amount of data that can be sent on the connection
changes.evidence: The initial maximum data parameter is an integer value that contains the initial value for the maximum amount of data that can be sent on the connection.  This is equivalent to sending a MAX_DATA (Section 19.9) for the connection immediately after completing the handshake.

changes.change_condition: when receiving an outdated MAX_DATA frame
changes.change_action: must accept smaller value than previously seen
changes.old_value: one found in an older packet
changes.evidence: A receiver MUST accept packets containing an outdated frame, such as a MAX_DATA frame carrying a smaller maximum data value than one found in an older packet.

changes.change_condition: After a receiver has already advertised a limit for a stream
changes.change_action: smaller advertised value has no effect
changes.related_state_or_step: processing or issuing subsequent advertised limits
changes.evidence: Once a receiver advertises a limit for the connection or a stream, it is not an error to advertise a smaller limit, but the smaller limit has no effect.

changes.change_condition: To avoid blocking a sender
changes.change_action: may be sent multiple times within a round trip or sent early enough to allow time for loss of the frame and subsequent recovery
changes.evidence: To avoid blocking a sender, a receiver MAY send a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame multiple times within a round trip or send it early enough to allow time for loss of the frame and subsequent recovery.

changes.change_condition: When a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame does not increase flow control limits
changes.evidence: A sender MUST ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frames that do not increase flow control limits.

changes.change_condition: When a receiver advertises a larger limit for a stream
changes.change_action: set to maximum absolute byte offset of a stream
changes.new_value: maximum absolute byte offset of a stream
changes.evidence: A MAX_STREAM_DATA frame indicates the maximum absolute byte offset of a stream.

changes.change_condition: When determining the flow control offset to advertise for a stream
changes.change_action: derived/computed from current offset of data consumed on that stream
changes.new_value: flow control offset based on the current offset of data consumed on that stream
changes.evidence: A receiver could determine the flow control offset to be advertised based on the current offset of data consumed on that stream.

changes.change_condition: an endpoint receives more data than the largest maximum stream data that it has sent for the affected stream
changes.evidence: An endpoint MUST terminate a connection with an error of type FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR if it receives more data than the largest maximum stream data that it has sent for the affected stream.

changes.change_condition: before sending flow control credit updates
changes.change_action: must not be delayed until receipt of STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED
changes.evidence: Therefore, a receiver MUST NOT wait for a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED or DATA_BLOCKED frame before sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA or MAX_DATA frame;

changes.change_condition: counting data toward this limit
changes.new_value: accounts for the largest received offset of data that is sent or received on the stream
changes.evidence: When counting data toward this limit, an endpoint accounts for the largest received offset of data that is sent or received on the stream.

changes.change_condition: for a stream in the "Data Sent" state when MAX_STREAM_DATA frames are received from the peer
changes.evidence: The endpoint can safely ignore any MAX_STREAM_DATA frames it receives from its peer for a stream in this state.

changes.change_condition: initial per-stream flow control transport parameter is absent
changes.evidence: If the transport parameter is absent, streams of that type start with a flow control limit of 0.

changes.change_condition: initial per-stream flow control transport parameter is present
changes.new_value: equivalent to sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame on every stream of the corresponding type immediately after opening
changes.evidence: If present, transport parameters that set initial per-stream flow control limits (initial_max_stream_data_bidi_local, initial_max_stream_data_bidi_remote, and initial_max_stream_data_uni) are equivalent to sending a MAX_STREAM_DATA frame (Section 19.10) on every stream of the corresponding type immediately after opening.

changes.change_condition: sending data on a stream
changes.new_value: the largest maximum stream data value advertised by the receiver
changes.evidence: The data sent on a stream MUST NOT exceed the largest maximum stream data value advertised by the receiver.

changes.change_condition: when determining how large a limit is advertised
changes.change_action: small changes are undesirable if sent frequently; larger increments to limits are necessary if updates are less frequent
changes.evidence: Therefore, frequently sending MAX_STREAM_DATA and MAX_DATA frames with small changes is undesirable.  On the other hand, if updates are less frequent, larger increments to limits are necessary to avoid blocking a sender, requiring larger resource commitments at the receiver.

changes.change_condition: when sending a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame
changes.new_value: the offset of the stream at which the blocking occurred

Maximum Stream Data:  A variable-length integer indicating the offset

changes.change_condition: when the packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAM_DATA frame for a stream is lost or when the limit is updated
changes.change_action: set to current value / updated value
changes.new_value: The current maximum stream data offset
changes.evidence: The current maximum stream data offset is sent in MAX_STREAM_DATA frames.  Like MAX_DATA, an updated value is sent when the packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAM_DATA frame for a stream is lost or when the limit is updated

changes.change_condition: MAX_STREAMS frames that do not increase the stream limit
changes.evidence: MAX_STREAMS frames that do not increase the stream limit MUST be ignored.

changes.change_condition: Once a receiver advertises a stream limit using the MAX_STREAMS frame, advertising a smaller limit
changes.change_action: must not decrease effective value; smaller advertised value has no effect
changes.evidence: Once a receiver advertises a stream limit using the MAX_STREAMS frame, advertising a smaller limit has no effect.

changes.change_condition: a peer opens more streams than was permitted
changes.evidence: An endpoint MUST terminate a connection with an error of type STREAM_LIMIT_ERROR if a peer opens more streams than was permitted.

changes.change_condition: a received MAX_STREAMS frame has a lower stream limit than was previously received, or does not increase the stream limit
changes.evidence: MAX_STREAMS frames that do not increase the stream limit MUST be ignored.

changes.change_condition: a received MAX_STREAMS frame permits opening of a stream larger than this limit
changes.evidence: Receipt of a frame that permits opening of a stream larger than this limit MUST be treated as a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

changes.change_condition: encoding or advertising stream opening limit
changes.evidence: This value cannot exceed 2^60, as it is not possible to encode stream IDs larger than 2^62-1.

changes.change_condition: for STREAMS_BLOCKED frame field validation
changes.evidence: This value cannot exceed 2^60, as it is not possible to encode stream IDs larger than 2^62-1.

changes.change_condition: if the frame encodes a value larger than allowed
changes.evidence:
Receipt of a frame that encodes a larger stream ID MUST be treated as a connection error of type

changes.note: The text refers to a larger stream ID while discussing Maximum Streams.

changes.change_condition: opening streams under the current stream limit set by a peer
changes.new_value: the current stream limit set by its peer
changes.evidence: An endpoint MUST NOT open more streams than permitted by the current stream limit set by its peer.

changes.change_condition: when a packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAMS for a stream type frame is declared lost or when the limit is updated
changes.new_value: The limit on streams of a given type
changes.evidence: The limit on streams of a given type is sent in MAX_STREAMS frames.  Like MAX_DATA, an updated value is sent when a packet containing the most recent MAX_STREAMS for a stream type frame is declared lost or when the limit is updated

changes.change_condition: when sending a STREAMS_BLOCKED frame
changes.change_action: set to current maximum allowed number
changes.new_value: the maximum number of streams allowed at the time the frame was sent

Maximum Streams:  A variable-length integer indicating the maximum

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame includes an Offset field
changes.evidence: This field is present when the OFF bit is set to 1.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame type low-order bits
changes.change_action: must equal 1 for Offset field to be present
changes.evidence: The OFF bit (0x04) in the frame type is set to indicate that there is an Offset field present.  When set to 1, the Offset field is present.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame type low-order bits
changes.change_action: must equal 0 for Offset field to be absent
changes.evidence: When set to 0, the Offset field is absent and the Stream Data starts at an offset of 0

changes.change_condition: A Stream Data field has a length of 0
changes.new_value: the offset of the next byte that would be sent

changes.change_condition: CRYPTO frame exceeds maximum largest offset delivered on a stream
changes.new_value: must not cause the sum of the offset and data length to exceed 2^62-1
changes.evidence: The largest offset delivered on a stream -- the sum of the offset and data length -- cannot exceed 2^62-1.  Receipt of a frame that exceeds this limit MUST be treated as a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR or CRYPTO_BUFFER_EXCEEDED.

changes.change_condition: Offset field is absent in a STREAM frame
changes.evidence: When the Offset field is absent, the offset is 0.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame OFF bit is set to 0
changes.evidence: When set to 0, the Offset field is absent and the Stream Data starts at an offset of 0

changes.change_condition: When placing STREAM frame data in order
changes.change_action: used together with stream ID to determine data placement
changes.evidence: An endpoint uses the Stream ID and Offset fields in STREAM frames to place data in order.

changes.change_condition: a received STREAM frame exceeds the limit that the sum of the offset and data length cannot exceed 2^62-1
changes.evidence: Receipt of a frame that exceeds this limit MUST be treated as a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR or FLOW_CONTROL_ERROR.

changes.change_condition: client sends another series of Initial packets after the server sends a TLS HelloRetryRequest
changes.new_value: matching the size of the CRYPTO frames sent in the first flight of Initial packets

changes.change_condition: counting data toward Maximum Stream Data
changes.new_value: largest received offset of data that is sent or received on the stream
changes.evidence: When counting data toward this limit, an endpoint accounts for the largest received offset of data that is sent or received on the stream.

changes.change_condition: evaluating the largest offset delivered on a stream
changes.evidence: The largest offset delivered on a stream -- the sum of the offset and data length -- cannot exceed 2^62-1

changes.change_condition: first CRYPTO frame sent by the client in the first packet
changes.related_state_or_step: first CRYPTO frame in client's first packet
changes.evidence: The first CRYPTO frame sent always begins at an offset of 0;

changes.change_condition: for CRYPTO frames in each packet number space
changes.related_state_or_step: ordered delivery of cryptographic handshake data
changes.evidence: The offsets used by CRYPTO frames to ensure ordered delivery of cryptographic handshake data start from zero in each packet number space.

changes.change_condition: for computing final size
changes.change_action: Final Size is derived as one higher than the offset of the byte with the largest offset sent on the stream
changes.evidence: More generally, this is one higher than the offset of the byte with the largest offset sent on the stream, or zero if no bytes were sent.

changes.change_condition: for each encryption level CRYPTO data flow start
changes.related_state_or_step: separate flow of cryptographic handshake data in each encryption level
changes.evidence: There is a separate flow of cryptographic handshake data in each encryption level, each of which starts at an offset of 0.

changes.change_condition: in a CRYPTO frame
changes.change_action: must specify byte offset in the stream for the data in this CRYPTO frame
changes.evidence: Offset:  A variable-length integer specifying the byte offset in the stream for the data in this CRYPTO frame.

changes.change_condition: largest offset delivered on a stream computed for CRYPTO frame
changes.new_value: sum of the offset and data length
changes.evidence: The largest offset delivered on a stream -- the sum of the offset and data length -- cannot exceed 2^62-1.

changes.change_condition: receiving STREAM frames
changes.evidence: Receiving STREAM frames might not increase the largest received offset.

changes.change_condition: referring to the first byte in the stream
changes.evidence: The first byte in the stream has an offset of 0.

changes.change_condition: sending data on a stream
changes.change_action: must be less than Final Size
changes.evidence: An endpoint MUST NOT send data on a stream at or beyond the final size.
changes.note: The chunk expresses the constraint on sent data position relative to Final Size rather than directly naming Offset in the prohibition.

changes.related_state_or_step: send an ACK frame without delay

changes.new_value: one less than the length of the Packet Number field in bytes

changes.change_condition: in packet types that contain a Packet Number field

changes.change_condition: Before the server has both received a non-probing packet from the client at its preferred address and validated the new path.

changes.evidence: A client constructs packets using any previously unused active connection ID, taken from either the preferred_address transport parameter or a NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame.

changes.change_condition: Client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address.
changes.evidence: If a client receives packets from a new server address when the client has not initiated a migration to that address, the client SHOULD discard these packets.

changes.change_condition: If path validation fails.

changes.related_state_or_step: sending a 1-RTT packet on that path
changes.evidence: If the spin bit is enabled for the connection, the endpoint maintains a spin value for each network path and sets the spin bit in the packet header to the currently stored value when a 1-RTT packet is sent on that path.

changes.change_condition: packet type check

changes.change_condition: server processing spin value

changes.change_condition: To distinguish stream directionality

changes.evidence: The second least significant bit (0x02) of the stream ID distinguishes between bidirectional streams (with the bit set to 0) and unidirectional streams (with the bit set to 1).

changes.change_condition: To identify the initiator of the stream
changes.change_action: must equal bit pattern by initiator
changes.new_value: least significant bit 0 for client-initiated, 1 for server-initiated
changes.evidence: The least significant bit (0x01) of the stream ID identifies the initiator of the stream.  Client-initiated streams have even-numbered stream IDs (with the bit set to 0), and server-initiated streams have odd-numbered stream IDs (with the bit set to 1).

changes.evidence: A stream ID is a 62-bit integer (0 to 2^62-1) that is unique for all streams on a connection.

changes.change_condition: Within a connection
changes.evidence: A QUIC endpoint MUST NOT reuse a stream ID within a connection.

changes.evidence: The same stateless reset token MUST NOT be used for multiple connection IDs.

changes.change_condition: Address validation token integrity requirement
changes.evidence: An address validation token MUST be difficult to guess.

changes.change_condition: After revealing the stateless reset token

changes.change_condition: For stateless reset token generation
changes.evidence: The stateless reset token MUST be difficult to guess.

changes.change_action: should not be accepted multiple times

changes.evidence: Tokens that are provided in NEW_TOKEN frames (Section 19.7) need to be valid for longer but SHOULD NOT be accepted multiple times.

changes.change_action: must equal for stateless reset detection
changes.related_state_or_step: datagram validation against stateless reset token
changes.evidence: If the last 16 bytes of the datagram are identical in value to a stateless reset token

changes.change_condition: If token validation fails
changes.evidence: If the token is invalid, then the server SHOULD proceed as if the client did not have a validated address, including potentially sending a Retry packet.

changes.change_condition: Initial packet does not carry the expected token
changes.change_action: must equal expected token or packet may be discarded
changes.evidence: Servers MAY discard any Initial packet that does not carry the expected token.

changes.change_condition: Server acceptance policy to limit replay
changes.change_action: may be allowed to be used only once
changes.evidence: Servers are encouraged to allow tokens to be used only once, if possible;

changes.change_condition: Token-based validation scheme
changes.change_action: must be covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients
changes.evidence: the token MUST be covered by integrity protection against modification or falsification by clients.

changes.change_condition: Tokens sent in NEW_TOKEN frames
changes.change_action: must include information that allows verification that the client IP address has not changed from when the token was issued
changes.evidence: Tokens sent in NEW_TOKEN frames MUST include information that allows the server to verify that the client IP address has not changed from when the token was issued.

changes.change_condition: Tokens sent in Retry packets
changes.change_action: should include information that allows verification that source IP address and port in client packets remain constant
changes.evidence: Tokens sent in Retry packets SHOULD include information that allows the server to verify that the source IP address and port in client packets remain constant.

changes.change_condition: When a server receives an Initial packet with an address validation token
changes.change_action: must be validated unless address validation has already been completed
changes.evidence: When a server receives an Initial packet with an address validation token, it MUST attempt to validate the token, unless it has already completed address validation.

changes.change_condition: When comparing a datagram to stateless reset token values
changes.change_action: must be compared without leaking information about the value of the token
changes.evidence: When comparing a datagram to stateless reset token values, endpoints MUST perform the comparison without leaking information about the value of the token.

changes.change_condition: When producing the stateless reset token from the pseudorandom function output
changes.evidence: The output of this function is truncated to 16 bytes to produce the stateless reset token for that connection.

changes.change_condition: When selecting a token to use for a connection attempt
changes.change_action: selected from available tokens; sending the most recent unused token is most likely to be effective
changes.evidence: For a client, this ambiguity means that sending the most recent unused token is most likely to be effective.

changes.change_condition: all subsequent Initial packets after Retry
changes.new_value: value of the Token field from the Retry packet
changes.evidence: The value of the Token field is copied to all subsequent Initial packets;

changes.change_condition: attacker cannot generate a valid token for its own address and the client is able to return that token
changes.change_action: must be valid as proof to the server that it received the token
changes.evidence: As long as it is not possible for an attacker to generate a valid token for its own address (see Section 8.1.4) and the client is able to return that token, it proves to the server that it received the token.

changes.change_condition: client responds to a Retry packet with an Initial packet
changes.new_value: token provided in the Retry packet
changes.evidence: It also sets the Token field to the token provided in the Retry packet.

changes.change_condition: client retains an applicable and unused token for a server
changes.new_value: included in the Token field of its Initial packet
changes.evidence: When connecting to a server for which the client retains an applicable and unused token, it SHOULD include that token in the Token field of its Initial packet.

changes.change_condition: future connection using token from NEW_TOKEN frame
changes.related_state_or_step: client sending Initial packets in a future connection
changes.evidence: In a future connection, the client includes this token in Initial packets to provide address validation. The client MUST include the token in all Initial packets it sends, unless a Retry replaces the token with a newer one.

changes.change_condition: in a NEW_TOKEN frame
changes.evidence: The token MUST NOT be empty.

changes.change_condition: multiple NEW_TOKEN frames contain the same token value
changes.evidence: Clients are responsible for discarding duplicate values

changes.change_condition: processing an Initial packet containing a token that was provided in a Retry packet
changes.change_action: must not be used to send another Retry packet
changes.evidence: In response to processing an Initial packet containing a token that was provided in a Retry packet, a server cannot send another Retry packet; it can only refuse the connection or permit it to proceed.

changes.change_condition: receipt of a NEW_TOKEN frame with an empty Token field
changes.evidence: A client MUST treat receipt of a NEW_TOKEN frame with an empty Token field as a connection error of type FRAME_ENCODING_ERROR.

changes.evidence: If a server receives a client Initial that contains an invalid Retry token but is otherwise valid, it knows the client will not accept another Retry token.

changes.change_condition: server sends NEW_TOKEN frame
changes.change_action: must be unique across all clients
changes.evidence: A server MUST ensure that every NEW_TOKEN frame it sends is unique across all clients, with the exception of those sent to repair losses of previously sent NEW_TOKEN frames.

changes.new_value: expiration time in an encrypted form
changes.evidence: A server can store the expiration time or include it in an encrypted form in the token.

changes.change_condition: token from a NEW_TOKEN frame is considered for different connection attempts
changes.new_value: not reused for different connection attempts
changes.evidence: A client SHOULD NOT reuse a token from a NEW_TOKEN frame for different connection attempts.

changes.change_condition: token is not applicable to the server that the client is connecting to
changes.evidence: A client MUST NOT include a token that is not applicable to the server that it is connecting to, unless the client has the knowledge that the server that issued the token and the server the client is connecting to are jointly managing the tokens.

changes.change_condition: token issued with NEW_TOKEN
changes.change_action: must not include linkable information unless encrypted
changes.evidence: A token issued with NEW_TOKEN MUST NOT include information that would allow values to be linked by an observer to the connection on which it was issued. For example, it cannot include the previous connection ID or addressing information, unless the values are encrypted.

changes.change_condition: token obtained in a Retry packet
changes.change_action: must be used immediately during the connection attempt and must not be used in subsequent connection attempts
changes.evidence: In comparison, a token obtained in a Retry packet MUST be used immediately during the connection attempt and cannot be used in subsequent connection attempts.

changes.change_condition: token sent in the NEW_TOKEN frame
changes.evidence: Thus, a token SHOULD have an expiration time, which could be either an explicit expiration time or an issued timestamp that can be used to dynamically calculate the expiration time.

changes.change_action: must not be used for future connections
changes.evidence: The client MUST NOT use the token provided in a Retry for future connections.

changes.change_condition: unless a Retry replaces the token with a newer one
changes.related_state_or_step: client sending Initial packets after Retry
changes.evidence: The client MUST include the token in all Initial packets it sends, unless a Retry replaces the token with a newer one.

changes.evidence: INVALID_TOKEN (0x0b):  A server received a client Initial that contained an invalid Token field.

changes.change_condition: STREAM frame type encoding
changes.evidence: The Type field in the STREAM frame takes the form 0b00001XXX (or the set of values from 0x08 to 0x0f).

changes.change_condition: before completing the handshake
changes.evidence: Servers MUST NOT send a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame before completing the handshake.

changes.change_condition: for HANDSHAKE_DONE frames
changes.evidence: shows that HANDSHAKE_DONE frames have no content.

changes.change_condition: for RESET_STREAM frames
changes.evidence: RESET_STREAM Frame { Type (i) = 0x04, Stream ID (i), Application Protocol Error Code (i), Final Size (i), }

changes.change_condition: on receipt of a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame by a server
changes.evidence: A server MUST treat receipt of a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame as a connection error of type PROTOCOL_VIOLATION.
changes.note: invalid sender/receiver-role condition; catalog lacks a dedicated role field

changes.change_condition: when indicating ECN feedback in the ACK frame
changes.related_state_or_step: ECN Counts presence in ACK frame
changes.evidence: ECN counts are only present when the ACK frame type is 0x03.

changes.change_condition: when indicating reaching the bidirectional stream limit
changes.evidence: A STREAMS_BLOCKED frame of type 0x16 is used to indicate reaching the bidirectional stream limit

changes.change_condition: when indicating reaching the unidirectional stream limit
changes.evidence: a STREAMS_BLOCKED frame of type 0x17 is used to indicate reaching the unidirectional stream limit

when sending a HANDSHAKE_DONE frame

   }

when sending a STREAM_DATA_BLOCKED frame
When constructing a Stateless Reset to appear as a valid QUIC packet
changes.change_action: must include at least 38 bits of data
changes.new_value: at least 38 bits of data (or 5 bytes, less the two fixed bits)
changes.evidence: For the Stateless Reset to appear as a valid QUIC packet, the Unpredictable Bits field needs to include at least 38 bits of data (or 5 bytes, less the two fixed bits).

changes.change_condition: When generating a Stateless Reset
changes.change_action: set to values that SHOULD be indistinguishable from random
changes.new_value: values that SHOULD be indistinguishable from random
changes.evidence: The remainder of the first byte and an arbitrary number of bytes following it are set to values that SHOULD be indistinguishable from random.

changes.change_condition: Retry packet
changes.evidence: The value in the Unused field is set to an arbitrary value by the server; a client MUST ignore these bits.

changes.change_condition: server creating a Version Negotiation packet
changes.evidence: The value in the Unused field is set to an arbitrary value by the server.

changes.change_condition: where QUIC might be multiplexed with other protocols
changes.evidence: servers SHOULD set the most significant bit of this field (0x40) to 1 so that Version Negotiation packets appear to have the Fixed Bit field.

changes.change_condition: Example of provisional registration for an affected codepoint
changes.change_action: may be requested for a specific value despite stricter permanent-registration requirements
changes.evidence: For instance, a provisional registration for a frame type of 61 could be requested.

changes.change_condition: For codepoints that are encoded in variable-length integers, such as frame types
changes.evidence: For codepoints that are encoded in variable-length integers (Section 16), such as frame types, codepoints that encode to four or eight bytes (that is, values 2^14 and above) SHOULD be used unless the usage is especially sensitive to having a longer encoding.

changes.change_condition: For permanent registrations in a registry that identifies a special range, such as the QUIC Frame Types registry
changes.change_action: validated range membership for stricter policy
changes.evidence: For instance, the "QUIC Frame Types" registry (Section 22.4) has a stricter policy for codepoints in the range from 0 to 63.

changes.change_condition: For provisional registrations
changes.evidence:
All QUIC registries include the following fields to support provisional registration:

Value:  The assigned codepoint.

changes.change_condition: If any use of the codepoints is identified by the search or a request to update the registration is made
changes.evidence: If any use of the codepoints is identified by this search or a request to update the registration is made, the codepoint MUST NOT be reclaimed.

changes.change_condition: If no use of the codepoint was identified and no request was made to update the registration
changes.change_action: may be removed from the registry
changes.evidence: If no use of the codepoint was identified and no request was made to update the registration, the codepoint MAY be removed from the registry.

changes.change_condition: New requests for codepoints from QUIC registries
changes.change_action: selected from available values excluding certain values
changes.new_value: a randomly selected codepoint that excludes both existing allocations and the first unallocated codepoint in the selected space
changes.evidence: New requests for codepoints from QUIC registries SHOULD use a randomly selected codepoint that excludes both existing allocations and the first unallocated codepoint in the selected space.

changes.change_condition: Requests for multiple codepoints
changes.evidence: Requests for multiple codepoints MAY use a contiguous range.

changes.change_condition: When an application includes a requested codepoint as part of the registration, and the codepoint is unassigned and the requirements of the registration policy are met
changes.evidence: Applications to register codepoints in QUIC registries MAY include a requested codepoint as part of the registration.  IANA MUST allocate the selected codepoint if the codepoint is unassigned and the requirements of the registration policy are met.

changes.change_condition: When reclaiming provisional codepoints in a portion of the registry such as the 64-16383 range for codepoints that use variable-length encodings
changes.evidence: a portion of the registry (such as the 64-16383 range for codepoints that use variable-length encodings)

changes.change_condition: When using the first unassigned codepoint
changes.change_action: must be reserved for a specific allocation policy
changes.new_value: allocation using the Standards Action policy
changes.evidence: The use of the first unassigned codepoint is reserved for allocation using the Standards Action policy;

changes.change_condition: Example structure definition
changes.evidence: Variable-Length Field (8..24)

changes.change_condition: Clients use tokens obtained on one connection for another connection attempt
changes.evidence: Clients MAY use tokens obtained on one connection for any connection attempt using the same version.

changes.change_condition: Version Negotiation Packet
changes.evidence: Version (32) = 0,

changes.change_condition: Version Negotiation packet
changes.evidence: The Version field of a Version Negotiation packet MUST be set to 0x00000000.

changes.change_condition: for Version Negotiation packets
changes.change_action: must be validated using Destination Connection ID matching only
changes.evidence: Except for Initial and Stateless Resets, an endpoint only accepts packets that include a Destination Connection ID field that matches a value the endpoint previously chose.  This is the only protection offered for Version Negotiation packets.

changes.change_condition: for instance, when constructing a Version Negotiation packet
changes.evidence: For instance, an endpoint could include a reserved version in a Version Negotiation packet; see Section 17.2.1.

changes.change_condition: for packets matched to an existing connection
changes.change_action: invalid if packet indicates a different protocol version than that of the connection; packet is discarded
changes.evidence: Packets that are matched to an existing connection are discarded if the packets are inconsistent with the state of that connection.  For example, packets are discarded if they indicate a different protocol version than that of the connection

changes.change_condition: for packets with a supported version, or no Version field
changes.change_action: must have supported version or no Version field to be matched to a connection
changes.evidence: Packets with a supported version, or no Version field, are matched to a connection using the connection ID or -- for packets with zero-length connection IDs -- the local address and port.

changes.change_condition: for the version of QUIC defined in this document
changes.related_state_or_step: identification of the QUIC version in use
changes.evidence: The version of QUIC defined in this document is identified as 0x00000001

changes.change_condition: for this version of the specification
changes.evidence: This version of the specification is identified by the number 0x00000001.

changes.change_condition: for version negotiation representation
changes.evidence: The version 0x00000000 is reserved to represent version negotiation.

changes.change_condition: if a Version Negotiation packet lists the QUIC version selected by the client
changes.change_action: must not include selected value; packet must be discarded
changes.related_state_or_step: client processing of a Version Negotiation packet
changes.evidence: A client MUST discard a Version Negotiation packet that lists the QUIC version selected by the client.

changes.change_condition: if a client receives a Version Negotiation packet after it has received and successfully processed any other packet, including an earlier Version Negotiation packet
changes.change_action: invalid if value check fails; packet must be discarded
changes.related_state_or_step: processing a Version Negotiation packet during connection establishment
changes.evidence: A client MUST discard any Version Negotiation packet if it has received and successfully processed any other packet, including an earlier Version Negotiation packet.

changes.change_condition: if a client receives a packet that uses a different version than it initially selected
changes.change_action: must not equal the initially selected version; packet is discarded if different
changes.evidence: If a client receives a packet that uses a different version than it initially selected, it MUST discard that packet.

changes.change_condition: if a server receives a packet that indicates an unsupported version
changes.change_action: validated membership check against supported versions
changes.evidence: If a server receives a packet that indicates an unsupported version

changes.change_condition: if packets specify unsupported versions and are smaller than large enough to initiate a new connection for any supported version
changes.evidence: Servers MUST drop smaller packets that specify unsupported versions.

changes.change_condition: upon receipt by a client for identifying a Version Negotiation packet
changes.evidence: Upon receipt by a client, it will be identified as a Version Negotiation packet based on the Version field having a value of 0.

changes.change_condition: when endpoints test that a peer correctly ignores unknown or unsupported versions in any field where such versions are ignored
changes.change_action: may be set to a reserved version
changes.related_state_or_step: test behavior for unknown or unsupported version handling
changes.evidence: Endpoints MAY add reserved versions to any field where unknown or unsupported versions are ignored to test that a peer correctly ignores the value.

changes.change_condition: when identifying QUIC versions
changes.evidence: QUIC versions are identified using a 32-bit unsigned number.

changes.change_condition: when protecting packets that carry the cryptographic handshake
changes.new_value: salt specific to the QUIC version
changes.evidence: The packets that carry the cryptographic handshake are protected with a key that is derived from this connection ID and a salt specific to the QUIC version.

changes.change_condition: when sending packets to test that a peer correctly discards the packet
changes.change_action: may be set to a reserved version
changes.evidence: Endpoints MAY send packets with a reserved version to test that a peer correctly discards the packet.

changes.change_condition: when the most significant 16 bits of the version number are cleared
changes.evidence: Versions with the most significant 16 bits of the version number cleared are reserved for use in future IETF consensus documents.

changes.change_condition: when the version follows the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a
changes.evidence: Versions that follow the pattern 0x?a?a?a?a are reserved for use in forcing version negotiation to be exercised -- that is, any version number where the low four bits of all bytes is 1010 (in binary).

changes.change_condition: when using reserved versions in fields that contain version numbers
changes.related_state_or_step: testing peer handling of unknown or unsupported versions
changes.evidence: Some version numbers (0x?a?a?a?a, as defined in Section 15) are reserved for inclusion in fields that contain version numbers.
